MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
GUI (C++)

The majority of the examples in GUI folder are developed based on MFC, while others are developed based on IMGUI.

You can preview the captured audio and video and other feathers directly.

The GUI examples are as follows.

  1. AVCapture C++

    It demonstrates how to use APIs of Pro Capture Family Functions and Eco Capture Family Functions to capture and preview videos, capture, preview and render audios using interfaces of Pro/Eco Capture Family Universal Functions, record audio and video to MP4 files, and crop videos.

  2. AVCapture2 C++

    It demonstrates how to use APIs of Universal Functions to capture and render audio and video.

  3. HDRCapture C++

    It demonstrates how to use APIs to

    1. capture and render videos;
    2. preview HDR videos without an HDR display device;
    3. record specified part of video to an MP4 file.

  4. LowLatency C++

    It demonstrates how to use APIs to capture video in low-latency mode and compress the captured frames into JPEG using part time notification mode.

  5. MultiAudioCapture C++

    It demonstrates how to use APIs of Pro/Eco Capture Family Universal Functions to capture up to 8-channel LPCM audio and compressed audio.

  6. MultiStreaming C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to capture 3 video streams from the same channel, and preview in 3 windows. That is, to capture video 3 video streams simultaneously and render them separately.

  7. MWCCPlayer_DRLib C++

    It demonstrates how to use APIs of Pro/Eco Capture Family Universal Functions to capture and render signals with CC (Closed Caption). For details, refer to Closed Caption.

  8. MWCCPlayer_DShow C++

    It demonstrates how to use DShow to capture and render signals with CC (Closed Caption). For details, refer to Closed Caption.

  9. NDISender C++

    It demonstrates how to transmit the captured NDI streams within LAN using NDI library functions.

  10. OSDPreview C++

    It demonstrates how to use APIs to add OSD for Pro Capture Family devices.

  11. XICaptureQuad C++

    It demonstrates how to use APIs to capture and preview multi-channel videos simultaneously (up to 4 channels).

  12. MWVencEncode C++

    It demonstrates how to use APIs to encode the captured data using hardware instead of software.

  13. av_view

    It demonstrates how to capture and play the audio and video.

  14. av_view_sharpen

    It demonstrates how to sharpen your video.

  15. low_latency_view

    It demonstrates the process of low latency capture, and comparison of the low-latency with normal capture.
